# braintrustdata_scores (Data Source)

Lists Braintrust project scores using API-native filters.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List scores with API-native filters
data "braintrustdata_scores" "all" {
  project_id = "proj-123"
  limit      = 50
}

# Filter scores by exact name and type
data "braintrustdata_scores" "filtered" {
  project_name = "example-project"
  score_name   = "quality"
  score_type   = "categorical"
}

output "all_score_ids" {
  value = data.braintrustdata_scores.all.ids
}

output "filtered_scores" {
  value = data.braintrustdata_scores.filtered.scores
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `ending_before` (String) Optional pagination cursor to fetch scores before this ID.
- `filter_ids` (List of String) Optional list of score IDs to filter by. Maps to repeated `ids` query parameters.
- `limit` (Number) Optional max number of scores to return.
- `org_name` (String) Optional organization name filter.
- `project_id` (String) Optional project ID filter.
- `project_name` (String) Optional project name filter.
- `score_name` (String) Optional exact score name filter.
- `score_type` (String) Optional score type filter.
- `starting_after` (String) Optional pagination cursor to fetch scores after this ID.

### Read-Only

- `ids` (List of String) List of returned score IDs.
- `scores` (Attributes List) List of scores. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--scores))

<a id="nestedatt--scores"></a>
### Nested Schema for `scores`

Read-Only:

- `categories` (String) The score categories as a JSON-encoded string.
- `config` (String) The score configuration as a JSON-encoded string.
- `created` (String) The timestamp when the score was created.
- `description` (String) Description of the score.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the score.
- `name` (String) The name of the score.
- `position` (String) LexoRank position of the score within the project.
- `project_id` (String) The project ID that owns the score.
- `score_type` (String) Type of the score.
- `user_id` (String) The ID of the user who created the score.
